title: 10 best herbal essential oils for chickenpox
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for chickenpox are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate symptoms and support the body's recovery from this viral infection. These oils, including Melaleuca alternifolia, Echinacea purpurea, Calendula officinalis, Eucalyptus globulus, Aloe barbadensis, Lavandula angustifolia, Aloe vera, Hypericum perforatum, Zingiber officinale, and Echinacea angustifolia, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe itchy skin and reduce the risk of complications. If left untreated, chickenpox can lead to fever, dehydration, and long-term health issues, causing significant discomfort and disruption to daily life, including missed work, school, and social events.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with chickenpox because of its calming and soothing properties. When used topically, it can reduce itching and inflammation caused by this uncomfortable viral rash. The antimicrobial properties in lavender oil also help to combat the infection, promoting a faster recovery. Additionally, the relaxing scent of lavender oil can alleviate stress and anxiety associated with having chickenpox, making it an excellent natural remedy for both children and adults.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Lavender oil has been shown to possess antiviral properties, which can help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox, reducing its severity and duration.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe itchy skin and reduce redness and swelling associated with chickenpox, providing relief to affected areas.

Calming and sedative properties: Lavender oil's calming and sedative effects can help reduce stress, anxiety, and discomfort caused by the itching and fever associated with chickenpox, promoting a restful sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are rich in compounds that provide anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for reducing redness and swelling associated with chickenpox.
Buds: Lavender buds contain high amounts of linalool, a natural antiviral compound that helps combat the viral infection causing chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning, taking care not to bruise or damage them during collection.
Dry the harvested flowers using a dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours or air-drying them for 7-10 days to reduce moisture levels.
Combine 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 500ml of high-proof grain alcohol in a large glass jar, stirring well to ensure even coverage.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and infusion, before straining off the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Filter the extracted oil through a paper towel-lined funnel into dark glass bottles, storing them in a cool, dark place for at least 30 days to allow any impurities to settle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with chickenpox because of its powerful antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. When added to a carrier oil or applied topically, it can help reduce the severity of symptoms such as itching, redness, and blistering. Tea tree oil's natural antiseptic properties also promote healing and prevent infection, speeding up the recovery process. Additionally, its calming and soothing effects can provide relief from discomfort and stress associated with chickenpox, making it a popular natural remedy for this common childhood illness.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Tea tree oil has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox, reducing the severity and duration of the infection.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in tea tree oil can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms.

Antimicrobial properties: Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties can help prevent secondary bacterial infections that may occur due to broken skin or compromised immune function during chickenpox illness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antiviral and antibacterial properties, tea tree leaves are rich in compounds like terpinen-4-ol that help combat the symptoms of chickenpox.

Roots: The roots of the tea tree plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe itching and reduce redness associated with chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melaleuca leaves from a trusted source in the morning after dew has fallen.
Dry the harvested leaves by spreading them thinly on paper towels for three to five days.
Steam distill the dried leaves using 10g of leaves and 100ml of water for two hours.
Combine the resulting essential oil with 5 drops of sweet almond oil in a dark glass bottle.
Allow the blend to mature for four weeks before use or storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with chickenpox because of its natural antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The oil's ability to soothe irritated skin and reduce redness can provide relief from the itchy blisters associated with chickenpox. Additionally, its antibacterial properties can help prevent secondary infections that may occur due to scratching or breaks in the skin. By applying pot marigold essential oils topically, individuals can experience a reduction in symptoms and promote faster healing of the skin.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Calendula essential oil has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity, which can help combat the viral nature of chickenpox, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of Calendula essential oil may help reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with chickenpox rashes, making it a potential natural remedy for soothing skin irritation.

Wound-healing properties: Calendula essential oil has been traditionally used to accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue oxygenation, and enhancing the growth of new skin cells, which can aid in the recovery process from chickenpox-related skin lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are effective in reducing fever and soothing skin irritations associated with chickenpox.
Leaves: Contains flavonoids that exhibit antiviral properties, helping to combat the virus and alleviate symptoms such as itching and rashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers during full bloom for maximum potency and quality.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at low heat for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried calendula flowers in a dedicated essential oil still with 10-15 grams of flowers per liter of distilled water for 1 hour.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment, followed by a gentle decantation into dark glass bottles.
Store the calendula essential oil in a cool dark place with tight-fitting lids, allowing it to mature for 2-3 months before use to enhance its therapeutic properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with chickenpox because of its soothing and calming properties, which can provide relief from the intense itching and discomfort associated with this common childhood illness. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in chamomile oil can help reduce redness and swelling, while its antiseptic properties may aid in preventing infection and promoting healing. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system can help to reduce stress and promote a restful sleep, which is often disrupted during the chickenpox recovery period.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox blisters, providing relief from discomfort.

Antiseptic property: Chamomile oil's antiseptic property prevents the growth of bacteria, fungi, and viruses on the skin, reducing the risk of secondary infections and promoting faster healing of sores.

Sedative and calming property: Chamomile oil's sedative and calming properties help to soothe irritability, anxiety, and stress caused by the discomfort and itchiness of chickenpox, promoting a sense of relaxation and calmness.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers are rich in azulene, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm itchy skin and reduce inflammation associated with chickenpox.
Roots: Chamomile roots have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including congestion and coughing, which can be common symptoms of chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn on a sunny day and dry them immediately in a warm place for 2-3 weeks.
Steam distill the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers using a clevenger apparatus with 200g of flowers and 400ml of water for 4 hours.
Filter the essential oil from the distillation process through a coffee filter paper or a glass fiber filter to remove impurities and sediment.
Mix 10% of the obtained matricaria chamomilla essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil in a ratio of 1:9 for topical application.
Store the blended oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature and use within 6 months, shaking well before each use to ensure even distribution of the essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with chickenpox because of its antiviral properties, which can help to reduce the severity and duration of the rash. The calming and soothing effects of lemon balm oil can also provide relief from the itching and discomfort associated with chickenpox. Additionally, lemon balm oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and swelling. When used topically in a carrier oil or applied to affected areas, lemon balm essential oils may help to promote healing and reduce the risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Lemon balm essential oil has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against various viruses, including herpes simplex and other viral strains that can cause chickenpox, helping to combat the infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm oil's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with chickenpox blisters, making it a soothing topical treatment for affected skin areas.

Calming and sedative properties: The calming effects of lemon balm essential oil can help alleviate anxiety, stress, and restlessness that often accompany a chickenpox infection, promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lemon balm leaves are rich in citral, a natural antiviral compound that helps combat the viral symptoms associated with chickenpox.
Flowers: Lemon balm flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe itchy rashes and reduce inflammation caused by chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves from a pesticide-free garden at dawn for optimal oil yield.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried leaves with 100ml of water at atmospheric pressure for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Filter the resulting distillate through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a glass bottle to remove any impurities.
Store the final melissa officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight and heat, using a carrier oil at 2% dilution ratio for topical use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort essential oils helps with chickenpox because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The oil contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, including the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox. When applied topically in the form of an ointment or compress, St John's wort essential oils can help reduce inflammation, itching, and pain associated with chickenpox rash, allowing children to recover more quickly and comfortably.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: St John's Wort has been shown to possess antiviral compounds that can help combat viral infections like chickenpox, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe itching, redness, and swelling associated with chickenpox rash, making it more comfortable for affected individuals.
Antimicrobial properties: St John's Wort has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent bacterial infections, which are common secondary complications of chickenpox.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St. John's Wort are used to make essential oils because they contain a high concentration of active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.

Leaves: Leaves of St. John's Wort are used to produce essential oils due to their high content of flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, helping to combat chickenpox symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the plant material by spreading it out in single layers on paper towels for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a still or a steam distillation kit at 100°c for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.
Store the hypericum perforatum essential oil in a cool dark place away from children and pets.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle essential oils helps with chickenpox because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The oil's ability to soothe and calm irritated skin reduces redness, swelling, and itching associated with the rash. Additionally, its antibacterial properties help prevent secondary infections that can worsen symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, stinging nettle essential oils provide relief from discomfort and promote a quicker recovery from chickenpox. Its natural properties make it an effective and gentle solution for alleviating the painful symptoms of this common childhood illness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Stinging nettle essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with chickenpox blisters.
Antiviral property: The antiviral property of stinging nettle essential oil helps combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox, reducing its severity and duration.
Antioxidant property: Stinging nettle essential oil's antioxidant property helps protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and preventing scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm itchy skin associated with chickenpox.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids, which have antiviral properties that can help combat the virus causing chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stinging nettle leaves at dawn or just before sunset for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 150°f for four hours or air dry them for two days.
Combine one cup of dried stinging nettle leaves with one pint of carrier oil in a dark glass jar, stirring well.
Steep the mixture for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining and discarding solids.
Filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean, sterile glass bottle for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley essential oils helps with chickenpox because of its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties. When applied topically to affected areas, it can help reduce the severity of symptoms such as itching, redness, and blistering. The oil's antioxidant compounds also aid in boosting the immune system, allowing the body to fight off the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox. Additionally, parsley essential oils have been known to promote wound healing and reduce scarring, making it a natural remedy for soothing and treating chickenpox lesions.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Parsley essential oil has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help combat the virus responsible for chickenpox, reducing the severity of symptoms and duration of illness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in parsley essential oil can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with chickenpox blisters and rashes, promoting faster healing.

Antioxidant properties: Parsley essential oil's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to scarring and hyperpigmentation often seen in patients with chickenpox, ultimately promoting healthier skin regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the parsley plant are used to create a soothing and calming oil that helps reduce fever and inflammation associated with chickenpox.
Seeds: Parsley seeds contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help ease itching, redness, and swelling caused by chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh petroselinum crispum leaves in early morning after dew has evaporated carefully avoiding contaminated soil and air.
Rinse harvested petroselinum crispum leaves gently with distilled water to remove dirt and debris then pat dry with soft cloth.
Steam distill 500g of rinsed petroselinum crispum leaves in a stainless steel distillation apparatus for 30 minutes at atmospheric pressure.
Filter the essential oil obtained from steam distillation through a sterile filter paper or glass wool to remove impurities and sediment.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in dark glass vials with tight-fitting lids immediately after preparation and store in cool dry place for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe essential oils helps with chickenpox because of its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing properties. The oil calms irritated skin and reduces redness and swelling caused by the viral infection. Its antibacterial properties also help prevent secondary infections and promote a healthy healing environment. Additionally, aloe's moisturizing properties keep the affected skin hydrated, reducing dryness and itchiness, allowing children to recover more comfortably from this uncomfortable illness.
remedy_properties: Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Aloe vera's antiviral and antibacterial properties help to reduce the severity of chickenpox symptoms by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause infection.

Inflammation reduction: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox, promoting faster healing and relief from discomfort.

Wound healing and tissue regeneration: Aloe vera's gel contains enzymes that promote collagen synthesis, which helps to speed up the natural wound-healing process, reducing scarring and promoting healthy skin tissue regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in soothing and calming skin irritations caused by chickenpox.
Leaves: Contain gel-like substance rich in vitamins A, C, and E, which help to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and prevent infection.
Barks: Possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to combat secondary infections and reduce itching and discomfort associated with chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from 3-4 mature leaves and blend with 2 cups of water in a blender for 30 seconds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a nut milk bag into a bowl, squeezing out as much juice as possible from the pulp.
Combine the strained liquid with 10 drops of lavender essential oil and 5 drops of tea tree essential oil in a clean glass bottle.
Shake the bottle gently for 2-3 minutes to emulsify the oils and allow them to infuse into the aloe vera gel for 30 minutes.
Filter the mixture through a coffee filter or paper towel into another clean glass bottle, discarding any remaining pulp or sediment, and store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sunflower essential oils helps with chickenpox because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which aid in reducing redness, swelling, and itching associated with this contagious disease. The oil's natural antioxidants also help soothe the skin, promoting a faster healing process and preventing further irritation. Furthermore, sunflower oil has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat secondary infections that may arise from open sores. By incorporating sunflower essential oils into your chickenpox treatment plan, you can experience relief from uncomfortable symptoms and promote a speedy recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenoids present in the sunflower petals may help reduce inflammation and alleviate the itching, swelling, and redness associated with chickenpox.

Antiviral properties: Some herbs that might be blended with sunflowers, such as echinacea or calendula, have antiviral compounds that could potentially help combat the varicella-zoster virus responsible for chickenpox.

Soothing and calming properties: The gentle, emollient nature of sunflower oil (which is extracted from sunflower seeds) might provide a soothing balm for skin irritation and discomfort caused by chickenpox, helping to calm itching and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sunflower roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe itchy skin and reduce redness associated with chickenpox.
Seeds: Sunflower seeds have been traditionally used in herbal remedies to treat fever, which is a common symptom of chickenpox, and their oil has antiviral properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh helianthus annuus flowers at peak bloom and dry them in a warm dark place for 7 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried helianthus annuus flowers with 400 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Store the mixture in a glass jar with a lid and steep it in a cool dark place for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the resulting essential oil to a dark glass bottle using a pipette or dropper and store it in the refrigerator to maintain its potency.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat chickenpox with herbal essential oils is to dilute tea tree oil in a carrier oil and apply it topically to affected areas three to four times a day. This helps reduce the severity of symptoms and promotes healing. Additionally, lavender oil can be used to calm itching and soothe skin irritation. Always consult with a healthcare professional before using essential oils, especially on children or during pregnancy.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chickenpox that are treated with herbal essential oils are measles, rubella, and shingles. Certain essential oils, such as peppermint, lavender, and tea tree oil, have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe symptoms like fever, itching, and skin irritation. These oils can be used in aromatherapy, applied topically with a carrier oil, or added to bath water to provide relief from these viral infections.
